executive_summary.verdict: CMCSA is trading well below fair value by ALL metrics. This is the biggest opportunity we found.
executive_summary.worst_method: Graham (always 0 - negative book value from M&A)
executive_summary.key_insight: 2025: Lynch $51 and DCF $71 vs Price $28. That's an 83-152% gap! FCF growing consistently.

graham.status: N/A - negative book value from M&A goodwill
graham.recommendation: Graham not applicable for CMCSA - negative book value from acquisitions

peter_lynch.recommendation: Rare opportunity - 83% upside to Lynch fair value

1Y.analysis: DCF $71 vs Price $28 = 152% below fair value!

5Y.analysis: DCF grew from $45 to $71 while price stagnated

10Y.analysis: Historically tracks DCF well. Now at extreme discount.

dcf.recommendation: DCF shows 152% upside - massive discount to intrinsic value

risk_factors.cord_cutting: Cable subscribers declining 5-7% annually. BUT broadband + Peacock growing.
risk_factors.debt: $95B debt from Sky deal. BUT FCF covers interest easily.
risk_factors.competition: Netflix, Disney+. BUT Comcast owns content (NBC/Universal).

bull_case.thesis: Market overreacts to cord-cutting. Broadband is a toll road. FCF machine.
